name: Native API Component
description: The ESPHome native API is used to communicate with clients directly, with a highly-optimized network protocol. Currently, only the ESPHome tool, Home Assistant and ioBroker use this native API.

config_entries.label: Encryption
config_entries.description: If present, encryption will be enabled for the API. Using encryption helps to secure the communication between the device running ESPHome and the connected client(s).

config_entries.label: Encryption key
config_entries.description: Pre-shared base64-encoded key for encrypting API traffic. Leave empty to let ESPHome generate one — Home Assistant will read it back during pairing.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID used for code generation.

config_entries.label: Actions
config_entries.description: A list of user-defined actions. See [User-defined Actions](https://esphome.io/components/api#api-device-actions).

config_entries.label: Batch Delay
config_entries.description: The delay time for batching multiple state update messages together to reduce network overhead. Lower values send updates sooner but use more network packets, while higher values batch more efficiently but add latency. Must be between `0ms` and `65535ms` (65.535 seconds). Defaults to `100ms`.

config_entries.label: Custom Services
config_entries.description: Enable compilation of custom API services for external components that use the C++ `CustomAPIDevice` class. Only needed when external components register their own services via the native API. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Homeassistant Services
config_entries.description: Enable compilation of Home Assistant service call support for external components that use the C++ `CustomAPIDevice::call_homeassistant_service()` or `CustomAPIDevice::fire_homeassistant_event()` methods. This is automatically enabled when using `homeassistant.service` or `homeassistant.event` actions, or the `homeassistant` platform for number or switch components. Only needs to be manually set when external components call Home Assistant services without using the built-in actions. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Homeassistant States
config_entries.description: Enable compilation of Home Assistant state subscription support for external components that use the C++ `CustomAPIDevice::subscribe_homeassistant_state()` method. This is automatically enabled when using any `homeassistant` platform components (sensor, binary_sensor, text_sensor, switch, or number). Only needs to be manually set when external components subscribe to Home Assistant states without using the built-in components. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Listen Backlog
config_entries.description: The maximum number of pending connections in the listen queue. Must be between 1 and 10. Defaults to `1` for ESP8266/RP2040, `4` for ESP32 and other platforms. Lower values use less memory but may reject connections during bursts.

config_entries.label: Max Connections
config_entries.description: The maximum number of simultaneous API connections allowed. Must be between 1 and 20. Defaults to:

config_entries.label: Max Send Queue
config_entries.description: The maximum number of messages that can be queued for sending per connection before the connection is dropped. Must be between 1 and 64. Defaults to:

config_entries.label: Port
config_entries.description: The port to run the API server on. Defaults to `6053`.

config_entries.label: Reboot Timeout
config_entries.description: The amount of time to wait before rebooting when no client connects to the API. This is needed because sometimes the low level ESP functions report that the ESP is connected to the network, when in fact it is not - only a full reboot fixes it. Can be disabled by setting this to `0s`. Defaults to `15min`.
